g.text: Read CR spec, status OCID, and delete intent
Get the OCI resource by known identifier
Resolve an existing OCI resource by display name
Persist the resolved or created OCID back into status
- Best-effort cleanup remains non-blocking and must stay
within the controller's eligible duplicate set.
- Implementation lookup also treats INACTIVE container
instances as existing matches even though that state is
Submit OCI delete for Container Instance
Confirm deletion with follow-up GetContainerInstance calls until the lifecycle is DELETED or the resource is not found.
Remove the finalizer after OCI deletion is confirmed
Request requeue and keep the finalizer
Return an unsuccessful terminal reconcile result
Compare live OCI state with the supported drift surface
Reject the change before any OCI mutation
Move the container instance compartment before reconciling supported tag or display-name drift.
Run duplicate-name cleanup as a separate non-blocking pass over eligible OCI resources.
Apply only the supported in-place update surface
Return success for the usable active state
